category: Our Team, National Panelist, Mediation Panelist, International Panelist
short_bio: Hon. Keshari Raj Pandit is a former Chief Judge of the High Court of Nepal. Hon. Pandit has decades of experience in Nepal’s Judicial and Legal system. He started his career at the Government of Nepal as a public prosecutor in 1978 and later on promoted as the Undersecretary at the Ministry of Law.
professional_background: Hon. Keshari Raj Pandit is a former Chief Judge of the High Court of Nepal. Hon. Pandit has decades of experience in Nepal’s Judicial and Legal system. He started his career at the Government of Nepal as a public prosecutor in 1978 and later on promoted as the Undersecretary at the Ministry of Law. He was appointed District Judge in 1989 and Judge of the High Court in 1996. At the time of his retirement from the Court in 2013, he was the Chief Judge of the High Court, Patan. He has also served in various other reputed positions such as the Executive Director of the National Judicial Academy, member of the Central Justice Sector Coordination committee at the Supreme Court of Nepal (2014-2018), Chairperson of Justice Sector Coordination Committee, Court of Appeal, Hetauda, and Patan (2008-2013) as well as Chairperson and Law Member of Revenue Tribunal Kathmandu, Nepal (2005-2008) among other prominent designations. He is a Life Member of Nepal Council of Arbitration (NEPCA) and also a Life Member of Nepal Mediators Society. Hon. Pandit holds a Master’s of Commerce and Bachelors of Law from Tribhuvan University, Diploma in International Law from Institute of Social Studies, the Hague, the Netherlands, and ‘Government Legal Advisors’ course at Institute of Advanced Legal Studies London. If you’d like a free consultation, please start by completing the form: Contact us at the NIAC office nearest to you or submit an inquiry online.

professional_background: Ms. Kabita Silwal is a forthcoming legal expert with her expertise in the field of commercial Law. She was formerly a part of Nepal Judicial Academy and currently involved in Judges Society where she gained valuable experience in judicial processes, training and institutional framework. She has a strong interest in the field of Alternative Dispute Resolution mechanisms and actively contributes in the development of ADR through her expedient approaches in resolving commercial disputes. She is committed to strengthening access to justice and enhancing dispute resolution practices in Nepal. Contact us at the NIAC office nearest to you or submit an inquiry online.

professional_background: Mr. Niraula is a Senior Advocate at the Supreme Court of Nepal and also an ADR Neutral specializing primarily on Arbitration and Mediation. short_bio: Mrs. Suneeta Regmi Pokhrel is a senior Advocate and certified mediator with more than 30 years of experience in Nepal’s legal system. Her area of expertise is property law, women’s rights and gender justice. She is an executive member of the Mediation Council (Supreme Court of Nepal) and the member
professional_background: Mrs. Suneeta Regmi Pokhrel is a senior Advocate and certified mediator with more than 30 years of experience in Nepal’s legal system. Her area of expertise is property law, women’s rights and gender justice. She is an executive member of the Mediation Council (Supreme Court of Nepal) and the member of the Professional Development Committee (Nepal Bar Council). Through these roles she has helped to improve mediation policy and professional legal standards. Mrs. Pokharel is a skilled mediator, trainer and legal educator. She has fascilitated many court referred and community mediations and taught many classes on gender, leadership and legal literacy.

short_bio: Prof. Dr. Rishikesh Wagle is the current Dean of Kathmandu University School of Law, Dhulikhel. He is a former District Court judge of Nepal and a faculty judge at the National Judicial Academy. He has worked in the Nepal Government from 1993 to 2019. Prof Wagle is a renowned judicial officer with e
professional_background: Prof. Dr. Rishikesh Wagle is the current Dean of Kathmandu University School of Law, Dhulikhel. He is a former District Court judge of Nepal and a faculty judge at the National Judicial Academy. He has worked in the Nepal Government from 1993 to 2019. Prof Wagle is a renowned judicial officer with excellent academic records. He has published a number of research papers on the Study of Supreme Court Judgements on Transitional Justice. He has published the book “Value Added Tax and Income Tax Laws of Nepal” showcasing his meticulous understanding of the Tax regime in Nepal. His more than 30 years of experience in the legal sector of Nepal is a treasure for NIAC. professional_background: Email: kkjha.multinepal@gmail.com kishorekjha1984@outlook.com Phone no: 98510-25600 ABOUT: Mr. Kishore Kumar Jha is a civil engineer and urban planner who has held numerous leadership positions in the Regional and Urban Planner’s Society of Nepal (RUPSON) AND Nepal Engineers’ Association (NEA). He has trained end number of engineers and provided policy and planning advice to government agencies. He is a feverent supporter of ADR in Nepal’s infrastructure industry, advocating for the use of arbitration and mediation to resolve conflict in projects and communities. His work advances social justice, sustainable development, and institutional integration of ADR in engineering and urban governance by fusing technical understanding with a strong social conscience.
